title: tirth8205/code-review-graph
friendlyTitle: code-review-graph helps AI focus on the right parts of a codebase
summary: code-review-graph is a local-first code intelligence graph for CLI and MCP workflows, designed to help coding agents retrieve the parts of a repository that matter for a change. The project has about 20,000 stars and led today’s collected AI-related GitHub Trending results by momentum.
friendlySummary: This popular open-source tool builds a local map of a software project. AI coding tools can use the map to read the files and relationships that matter for a task.
whyItMatters: Use it to build a persistent structural map of a large codebase before asking an agent to review or modify it. Its focused retrieval can reduce repeated whole-repository reads, but benchmark the context savings and review quality on your own languages and project shape.
friendlyWhyItMatters: Try it on a large project when an agent reads too much code or misses important links. Compare its results with your current review process before relying on it.
